title: She Thought the Lottery Scanner Was Broken. It Wasn't.
script: Broken scanner. Or $2.2 million? A 66-year-old woman from Charlevoix County, Michigan had an urge to buy a lottery ticket — Millionaire for Life from the Michigan Lottery. She scanned it at a store. A message came up: contact the lottery office. She figured the machine was broken. Walked to her car. Looked up the Michigan Lottery website. Her 5 numbers — 03, 15, 16, 24, 28 — matched the May 21 drawing. Not broken. She won $100,000 a year for life. She chose the lump sum: $2.2 million. The winning ticket was from the Mancelona North EZ Mart. She stayed anonymous. She's buying a house, a car, and a boat. If your lottery scanner said contact the lottery office, would you assume it was broken?
